audit_status.source_base: Greek Romans text tradition, draft aligned to public-domain comparison layer later
audit_status.review_note: Compact complete draft reviewed for opening, gospel, trust, righteousness/justice, wrath as unveiling, idolatry exchange, degraded patterns. Handle with care; do not weaponize.

verses.restored: Paul, servant of Messiah Jesus, called as apostle, set apart for the good news of the Divine,
which he promised beforehand through his prophets in holy Scriptures,
concerning his Son, who came from the seed of David according to flesh,
and was marked out Son of the Divine in power according to Spirit of holiness by resurrection of the dead: Jesus Messiah our Lord,
through whom we received grace and apostleship for obedience of trust among all the nations for his name,
among whom you also are called ones of Jesus Messiah,
to all those in Rome, beloved of the Divine, called holy ones: grace to you and peace from Divine our Father and Lord Jesus Messiah.
First, I thank my Divine through Jesus Messiah for all of you, because your trust is announced in the whole world.
For the Divine is my witness, whom I serve in my spirit in the good news of his Son, how unceasingly I make mention of you,
always in my prayers asking if somehow now at last I may be prospered in the will of the Divine to come to you.
For I long to see you, so that I may share with you some spiritual gift, to strengthen you,
that is, to be mutually encouraged among you through one another's trust, both yours and mine.
I do not want you to be unaware, siblings, that often I planned to come to you, and was prevented until now, so that I might have some fruit also among you, just as among the rest of the nations.
I am debtor both to Greeks and to non-Greeks, both to wise and to uncomprehending.
Thus, for my part, I am eager to announce the good news also to you who are in Rome.
For I am not ashamed of the good news; for it is power of the Divine for salvation/healing to everyone trusting, to Judean first and also to Greek.
For in it the righteousness/justice of the Divine is unveiled from trust into trust, just as it is written: The righteous/just one will live from trust.
For wrath of the Divine is unveiled from heaven against all ungodliness and injustice of humans who hold down the truth in injustice,
because what is knowable of the Divine is manifest among them, for the Divine made it manifest to them.
For the unseen things of him, both his everlasting power and divine nature, being understood from the created order, are perceived through what has been made, so that they are without excuse.
Because knowing the Divine, they did not glorify as Divine or give thanks, but became empty in their reasonings, and their uncomprehending heart was darkened.
Claiming to be wise, they became foolish,
and exchanged the glory of the imperishable Divine for a likeness of an image of perishable human and birds and four-footed creatures and reptiles.
Therefore the Divine handed them over in the desires of their hearts to uncleanness, to dishonor their bodies among themselves,
they who exchanged the truth of the Divine for the lie, and revered and served the creature rather than the Creator, who is blessed into the ages. Amen.
Because of this the Divine handed them over to dishonorable passions; for their females exchanged natural use for what is beyond/against nature,

and likewise also the males, leaving natural use of the female, were inflamed in their desire for one another, males with males working what is shameful and receiving in themselves the return that was fitting for their error.
And just as they did not approve to hold the Divine in knowledge, the Divine handed them over to an unapproved mind, to do things not fitting,
having been filled with every injustice, wickedness, greed, malice; full of envy, murder, strife, deceit, harmful character; whisperers,
slanderers, Divine-haters, insolent, arrogant, boastful, inventors of harmful things, disobedient to parents,
without understanding, covenant-breaking, without natural affection, without mercy.
These, knowing the righteous/just decree of the Divine, that those practicing such things are worthy of death, not only do them but also approve those practicing them.

safety_note: Romans 1 is presented as part of Paul's wider indictment of idolatrous exchange and distorted desire leading into Romans 2-3, where Paul turns the critique on the judging reader and establishes universal accountability. This draft should not be detached as a weapon against sexual minorities or used to flatten Paul's argument into anti-LGBT rhetoric.
